# Executive Brief — Sprint 5 (Connector Framework + Houzz Mining Automation)
**Date:** 2026-06-27 | **Generated by:** Claude Code (Autonomous Development Mode) | **Commit:** `8cc1701`

---

## What Was Built

### 1. Universal Connector Framework
A permanent SDK that all data integrations (Houzz, HubSpot, Outlook, QuickBooks, Google Drive, Procore, Autodesk) will share. Every new connector inherits:
- 7-stage pipeline: Discover → Validate → Normalize → Persist → Mine → Knowledge Graph → Executive Reporting
- Autonomous recovery: 3 retries with exponential backoff (no Buck involvement for transient failures)
- Incremental sync: only fetches records changed since last run (not full re-import each time)
- Sync state tracking in `connector_sync_state` table

### 2. Full Houzz Mining Automation (Reference Implementation)
Houzz Connector now covers **all 17 entity types** — the complete picture of every project:

| Module | Entity Type | What it captures |
|--------|-------------|-----------------|
| Projects | projects | Name, client, status, budget, dates |
| Field Log | daily_logs | Daily entries, weather, crew size |
| Schedule | schedule_items | Tasks, completion %, assignees |
| Photos/Files | files | Photos, documents, categories, rooms |
| Time Tracking | time_entries | Hours, worker, cost codes, billable |
| To-Do / Punch List | tasks | Open items, punch list, priority |
| Messages | messages | Client/team messages, threads |
| Financial Overview | budget | Budget vs. actual by category |
| Estimates | estimates | Status, amounts, approval dates |
| Contracts | contracts | Signed contracts, counterparty, amounts |
| Purchase Orders | purchase_orders | Vendors, PO amounts, delivery status |
| Change Orders | change_orders | Scope changes, pending approvals |
| Selections | selections | Client choices, allowances, actuals |
| Vendors | vendors | Trade vendors per project |
| Contacts | contacts | Client, designer, owner contacts |
| Team | team_members | Team with permission levels |
| Subs | subcontractors | License #, insurance expiry, trade |

### 3. Browser Agent Standard
A permanent protocol document (`BROWSER_AGENT_STANDARD.md`) that tells Browser Claude exactly what to extract, in what format, and where to send it. Includes:
- Canonical JSON schemas for all 17 entity types (copy-paste ready)
- Step-by-step 17-module extraction sequence
- API call examples (dry_run first, then write)
- Incremental sync protocol for subsequent runs

### 4. Architecture Documentation
3 Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) documenting the permanent architecture:
- ADR-001: Connector Framework design and schema changes
- ADR-002: Browser Agent as Discovery-Only agent (immutable rule)
- ADR-003: Generic Mining Pipeline design

## ROI Analysis

**Estimated time savings once data is loaded:**

| Use Case | Current (manual) | With HCI AI | Savings/week |
|----------|------------------|-------------|--------------|
| Change order status check | 15 min/week | 0 (dashboard) | 15 min |
| Insurance expiry monitoring | Manual calendar | Auto-alert | 30 min + risk |
| Budget vs. actual per category | 30 min/project | Instant | 90 min |
| Punch list completion tracking | Excel/Houzz tab | Dashboard | 20 min |
| Sub license/insurance audit | Quarterly manual | Continuous | 2 hrs/quarter |
| Client message follow-up | Manual Houzz check | Executive Inbox | 15 min/day |

**First extraction value (one-time):** ~4-6 hours of manual data collection per project, automated.

---

## Risk Items

| Risk | Severity | Mitigation |
|------|----------|-----------|
| Browser Agent extraction quality | MEDIUM | dry_run default + validation errors logged |
| Houzz session expiry during extraction | LOW | Browser Agent re-authenticates |
| ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE with expression index | MITIGATED | Fixed in migration — uses unique expression indexes |
| n8n webhook not yet configured | LOW | Connector works without it; webhook is optional Stage 4 |
